description: Goal: prove the activation partition model fixes the real observed runaway without local patches. Signal: cargo test --lib compiler2:: passes the quicksort frontier test and the Enum.reduce/range/list/fold-state-machine convergence tests; compiling quicksort under telemetry stays inside the agreed meaningful-event budget; hot helpers such as List.reduce_cont/3, Enum.reduce/3 bridge lambdas, and reducer closures have small intentional activation frontiers. Strategy: run the focused quicksort and compiler2 semantic/type sweeps under a hard timeout; inspect telemetry by function id/name; tighten tests around semantic meaning rather than type-id accidents; fix remaining overspecialization by improving partition policy only, not by naming fixtures or runtime helpers. Outro: no qsort-specific logic, no Enum.reduce-specific logic, no test-only budgets that ignore semantic event explosions, and no unchecked fz2 run that can consume unbounded memory.
title: Compiler2 convergence: burn down quicksort and Enum.reduce overspecialization
